The latest announcement is out from Xinjiang Xinxin Mining Industry Co ( (HK:3833) ).

Xinjiang Xinxin Mining Industry Co. has convened its 2025 annual general meeting for 22 May 2026 in Urumqi, Xinjiang, where shareholders will review the directors’ report and the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025. The meeting will also consider a proposed final dividend of RMB0.05 per share and the re-appointment of BDO China SHU LUN PAN Certified Public Accountants LLP as auditor, with share registration arrangements set to determine eligibility to attend.

The AGM timetable and closure of the register of members establish key dates for investors who wish to participate in the meeting and vote on the proposed resolutions. These include approval of profit distribution, dividend payment, and auditor remuneration, decisions that will influence shareholder returns and the continuity of the company’s external audit oversight.

More about Xinjiang Xinxin Mining Industry Co

Xinjiang Xinxin Mining Industry Co., Ltd. is a joint stock limited company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong. The company operates in the mining sector and is headquartered in Xinjiang, focusing on resource extraction and related activities within the PRC market.

data.title: Agile Group to Exit Project Company in RMB95 Million Asset-Backed Disposal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Agile Group Holdings ( (HK:3383) ) has issued an update.

Agile Group Holdings has agreed to sell its equity interest in a project company via an indirect, non-wholly owned subsidiary, exiting the entity entirely and removing it from the group’s consolidated financial statements. The RMB95 million consideration will be settled through assignment of debt that will, in turn, be repaid by transferring specified target properties to Agile, with the purchaser pledging its remaining 50% stake in the project company as security.

Under the deal, the project company will transfer target properties to Agile to settle the assigned debt, and Agile must dispose of these properties within six months, retaining the net sale proceeds. Any properties unsold after six months will still be transferred to Agile, ensuring value recovery, while the transaction is classified as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, signaling a moderate but not transformational shift in Agile’s project portfolio and risk exposure.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3383) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.40 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Agile Group Holdings stock, see the HK:3383 Stock Forecast page.

More about Agile Group Holdings

Agile Group Holdings is a Hong Kong-listed property developer operating through subsidiaries that invest in and manage real estate project companies. The group structures its holdings via indirect, non-wholly owned units to develop, hold, and dispose of residential and commercial properties in mainland China, focusing on optimizing its portfolio and capital structure through selective acquisitions and divestments.

Wai Hung Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3321) ) has provided an announcement.

Zhongke Group Holdings Limited has secured shareholder approval at its extraordinary general meeting on 22 April 2026 for a share consolidation, with every ten existing ordinary shares merged into one consolidated share, following a unanimous poll vote. The meeting, attended by most board members and overseen by an independent scrutineer, reported no voting restrictions, abstentions or opposition among shareholders.

Subject to final listing approval from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, the share consolidation will take effect on 24 April 2026, with trading in consolidated shares commencing the same day. The company will also change its board lot size on 11 May 2026 from 2,500 existing shares to 10,000 consolidated shares, and will issue new green share certificates, a move aimed at standardising trading units and potentially improving market liquidity and trading efficiency for investors.

More about Wai Hung Group Holdings Ltd.

Zhongke Group Holdings Limited, formerly known as Wai Hung Group Holdings Limited, is a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 3321. The group operates with a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, and has a substantial shareholder base with 726,696,000 issued shares prior to the latest corporate actions.

data.title: Xin Yuan Enterprises Replaces Forensic Investigator Amid Ongoing Probe and Trading Suspension
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Xin Yuan Enterprises Group Ltd. ( (HK:1748) ) has provided an update.

Xin Yuan Enterprises Group Ltd. has replaced its previously appointed independent forensic investigator after failing to reach agreement on the timetable for its ongoing forensic investigation into allegations involving the company and its directors. The special committee has appointed a new external independent accounting firm to continue and supplement the investigation and says there are no unresolved issues with the outgoing investigator.

The board emphasized that the change in forensic investigator will not alter the overall direction of the forensic probe, the internal control review or the timetable for clearing its backlog of financial results. Trading in the company’s shares, suspended since February 2025 pending satisfaction of resumption guidance from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, will remain halted, and shareholders and potential investors are urged to exercise caution when dealing in the company’s securities.

More about Xin Yuan Enterprises Group Ltd.

Xin Yuan Enterprises Group Ltd., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, is a company whose shares have been suspended from trading since February 2025 amid allegations against the firm and its directors. The board set up a special investigation committee, engaged independent legal and forensic experts, and is working toward fulfilling regulatory resumption guidance and publishing outstanding financial results.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Sun.King Technology Group Limited ( (HK:0580) ) has shared an announcement.

Sun.King Technology Group Limited has convened its annual general meeting for 2 June 2026 in Beijing, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements and directors’ and auditors’ reports for the financial year ended 31 December 2025. The meeting will also consider a proposed final dividend of HK$0.01 per share for 2025 and the re-election of three retiring directors, with the board to set director remuneration and seek reappointment of Ernst & Young as auditor.

Shareholders will vote on granting the board a broad share issuance mandate, allowing directors to allot, issue, or deal in shares and related convertible securities up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, during the authorised period. This general mandate, consistent with common Hong Kong market practice, is intended to give the company flexibility to raise capital, manage its equity structure, and respond to potential financing or strategic opportunities, which could influence future dilution levels and capital management for existing investors.

More about Sun.King Technology Group Limited

China Everbright Water Limited reported that all resolutions proposed at its annual general meeting on 22 April 2026 were duly approved by shareholders via poll voting. Ordinary resolutions passed included the adoption of the directors’ statement and audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025, the declaration of a final one-tier tax-exempt dividend of HK$0.0422 per share, and approval of directors’ fees of SGD349,277 for the financial year, underscoring strong shareholder support for the company’s governance, capital return and board remuneration decisions.

The near-unanimous backing of these items signals continued confidence in the company’s financial performance and strategic direction among investors. It focuses on investment, construction and operation of water treatment and related environmental infrastructure projects, serving municipal and industrial clients in Greater China and the region.

Ximei Resources Holding Limited has rescheduled its 2026 annual general meeting from 27 May to 29 June 2026 in Hong Kong due to administrative reasons, and adjusted the related book closure period for determining shareholders’ eligibility to attend and vote. China Treasures New Materials Group Limited. ( (HK:2439) ) just unveiled an update.

China Treasures New Materials Group has completed a placing of 200 million new shares under its general mandate, increasing its issued share capital from 1 billion to 1.2 billion shares. The placing shares, representing 16.67% of the enlarged capital, were allocated to at least six independent investors, none of whom became a substantial shareholder.

The transaction raises net proceeds of about HK$45.8 million for the company, earmarked for uses previously outlined by management and expected to support its ongoing business initiatives. Following the placing, controlling shareholders saw their stakes diluted but remain significant holders, while the proportion of public and institutional ownership in the company has expanded.

More about China Treasures New Materials Group Limited.

China Treasures New Materials Group Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged in the new materials sector. The group focuses on advanced materials and related technologies, serving industrial customers and positioning itself within China’s broader push toward higher-value, environmentally oriented manufacturing.
